title: Moroccan Sellou Delight
description: Sellou is a traditional Moroccan dish made from flour, honey, and almonds. This recipe is a delicious twist on the classic, with a tangy twist.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the sliced almonds, sellou, honey, tahini, pistachios, rose water, cinnamon, and salt. Mix well until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Shape the dough into small balls, about 1-inch in diameter. Place the balls onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the unsalted butter over the balls, making sure they're evenly coated.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the sellou is lightly golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
